body.text: signify the same; (T, S, O, L, K;) [The
a claim which one seeks to obtain for an injury,
(T, O, L, K; and so the Msb in explanation of the former word:) the former is also explained as signifying
due, annexed to property, claimed from the possessor of the property:

zamana Alt~aqaH~umi waAlmajaAEaho 
                      
                     
                        A^akalato Haniyfapu rab~ahaA
                      
                     ↓ 
      suwo'a AlEawaAqibi waAlt~ibaAEaho 
                      
                     
                        lamo yaHo*aruwA mino rab~ihimo
Haneefeh ate their lord, in the time of experiencing dearth,
sterility, and hunger: they did not fear, from their lord, the evil of the results, and the consequence
of their action]: for they had taken to themselves a god consisting of
, [i. e. dates mixed with clarified butter and the preparation of milk called
, kneaded together,] and worshipped it for some time; then famine befell them, and they ate it. (S.) And one says,

body.text_bw: maA Ealayohi mina A@ll~`hi fiY h`*aA tabiEapN
body.text_ar: مَا عَلَيْهِ مِنَ ا@للّٰهِ فِى هٰذَا تَبِعَةٌ

body.text: There is not, against him, on the part of God, in this, any claim on account of wrong-doing.
